title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale — Sandipan Bhaumik, Databricks
summary: Sandipan Bhaumik from Databricks presents five pillars for deploying agents at enterprise scale: evaluation, observability, data foundation, multi-agent orchestration, and governance. He illustrates the framework through a case study where a retail bank's failed chatbot was salvaged by investing six weeks in tracing and measurement infrastructure before model selection, and later caught a critical production bug through observability when policy documents became stale.
writeup: Sandipan Bhaumik of Databricks lays out five pillars for deploying AI agents at enterprise scale: evaluation, observability, data foundation, multi-agent orchestration, and governance. The framework comes from a retail bank engagement where six weeks of evaluation and tracing infrastructure preceded model selection, and where post-launch monitoring caught stale data issues after policy changes. The takeaway is that evaluation and observability are prerequisites rather than afterthoughts, and that ongoing monitoring is needed to detect data drift when underlying policies shift.
